title: Field Interface Codes
text: Field Interface Codes A field interface code is specified in the field's Database Interface property, and determines how the data is stored in the database. The code defines the packing code and field length, as well numeric decimal and scaling options. PackingCode { Length }{ . n }{ ( TotalLength ) } V Type / FieldId / SubfieldId / Separator Example: I8.4 (8-byte integer, scaled to 4 decimal places) Table 1. Syntax for Field Interface Definitions Code Meaning PackingCode Packing code, such as C, B2, M2, I8, W*. For more information, see Packing Codes . Packing codes determine how the data should be presented to the DBMS connector for packing and storing in the DBMS. Some packing codes are associated with a default field layout. For more information, see Default Packing Codes and Display Formats per Data Type . Length Length of each subfield in the field. If subfields are not defined, Length is the total field length. .n Optional decimal point and scaling. TotalLength Total length of field if subfields are defined. V Constant that indicates where the variable part of the definition begins. Type Variable-length identifier type. FieldId Field identifier. SubfieldId First subfield identifier. Separator Subfield separator. Related Links Database Interface (Fields) This property (also known as the field interface) determines how data in the field is physically stored in the database.
